candidate_workflow_target: Every AI-like request becomes bounded Universal Work and returns Candidate Artifact / Response Packet, not direct app mutation or external sends.
connected_apps_target: Show connected apps, app permissions, last request, disconnect/add app bridge status; app sovereignty remains mandatory.
dev_mode_target: Dev Mode exposes traces, receipts, proof gaps, validators, support bundle output, and raw JSON without granting apply, app-state, external-send, provider, or model authority.
handoff_first_flow: Raw request / file packet / agent packet / app event / QIRC event / demo trigger
handoff_first_hard_boundaries: Handoff-First does not bypass Universal Work
Handoff-First does not bypass Final Gate
handoff artifacts are candidate-only and evidence-bound
no QIRC event treated as truth
no external Thor/Y/YNode/Mjölnir runtime proof claimed
handoff_first_pre_intake_target: Handoff-First Pre-Intake Layer is Odin's first orientation layer for every raw app/user/file/agent/QIRC/demo input. It derives or ingests a structured Handoff Context before Universal Work compilation, then Universal Work bounds the request before Context Distillery, Worklets, Slots, Gaptext, ModelWorkPacket creation, smallest sufficient worker routing, critics/gates, Candidate Artifact/Response Packet return, Trace/Receipt capture, and app-owned decision. Handoff Context captures intent, task shape, source context, relevant artifacts, constraints, allowed outputs, forbidden actions, privacy boundary, model policy hints, expected candidate shape, proof/receipt expectations, return contract, known gaps, and handoff profile. It improves local LLM performance by reducing prompt chaos and producing better Universal Work and ModelWorkPackets. Handoff-First is mandatory for 100%; Generic and Thor profiles are mandatory; Y and Mjölnir profiles are mandatory structured profile contracts, not external runtime proof.

handoff_first_profiles.generic_handoff_profile: mandatory neutral fallback profile for ordinary user/app/file/QIRC/demo work
handoff_first_profiles.mjolnir_handoff_profile: mandatory structured Mjölnir/Mjolnir-style focused patch/build/engine-prep profile contract; not target-host, Godot, Windows, or external Mjölnir runtime proof
handoff_first_profiles.thor_handoff_profile: mandatory advisory handoff/review/guard/expected/return discipline profile for agents and code work; repo evidence is docs/schema/shadow candidate only unless locally proven later
handoff_first_profiles.y_handoff_profile: mandatory structured Y-family app/game/web/space/build handoff profile contract; not YNode runtime proof and not external product integration proof

handoff_first_required_formula: Handoff orients. Universal Work bounds. Odin gates. QIRC coordinates. Apps decide. Models work only as bounded workers.
ki_ohne_ki_target: Deterministic precompute, cache, rules, slots, contracts, validators, critics, and gates should shape work before model execution and remain visible enough that normal users and developers can see why model work was avoided or bounded.
llm_agent_target: LLMs and agents should receive bounded ModelWorkPackets, context capsules, route decisions, receipt boundaries, and candidate-only response contracts so 3B/7B/8B local models and advisory agents have more useful leverage without authority creep.
model_picker_target: Model choices include Test/Mock, Fast local, Better local, Strong local, and Remote explicit only disabled by default. No hidden fallback.
non_goals: Windows target host proof for handoff profiles
handoff raw internals exposed to normal users by default
normal_user_target: Odin is a local background engine / Local Runtime Hub. Normal users clone or download it, run one simple start command, open or are given a localhost Browser Hub, see status, choose model/provider modes, connect apps, submit demo Universal Work, receive Candidate Artifacts / Response Packets, read plain-language activity, and stop cleanly.
proof_gap_target: Proof gaps are first-class UI and Dev Mode objects; claims require scoped receipts.
q_shabang_target: Preserve Q-Shabang: Universal Work, Candidate Law, QIRC/Semantic Bus, Context Distillery, Worklets, Slot Forge, Gaptext, critics, gates, receipts, Thor handoff discipline, and QoOO-style smallest sufficient worker routing.
qirc_cognitive_substrate_cluster_target: QIRC is the local IRC-centered coordination core of Odin. In the narrow technical sense, QIRC Core means a localhost-only IRC/QIRC-compatible server/runtime, a semantic Odin channel registry, local event routing, and worker/model/app/candidate/trace/receipt/proof-gap channels. In the broader Odin sense, QIRC means QIRC Core plus a local cognitive substrate cluster: File/Spool, CLI/Pipe, Browser Events, SDK/App Bridge, Trace/Receipt channels, and Dev Mode visibility. These surrounding systems are KI-ohne-KI support substrates, not LLMs. They support local LLMs indirectly by providing context structure, durable work queues, thread/history continuity, source intake, handoff packet flow, event routing, replay, traceability, receipts, proof gaps, and app/agent bridge packet exchange. Required formula: QIRC coordinates. Odin gates. Apps decide. Models work only as bounded workers.
qirc_required_formula: QIRC coordinates. Odin gates. Apps decide. Models work only as bounded workers.
runtime_target: Localhost-only start/check/stop runtime surface, no hidden remote fallback, provider/model execution only when explicitly configured and receipted.
ui_target: Simple Browser Hub with Home, Models, Apps, Activity, and Dev Mode surfaces. Apps provide real app UX; Odin provides engine status, candidate workflow, diagnostics, receipts, proof gaps, and bridge behavior.
